title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: December 31, 2026
content:
The credit union remains compliant in most areas with board-approved policy limits, supported by strong capital and asset-quality data.

Asset Quality:
Classified assets/Net worth of 3% remain(s) below the limit of <40%, while delinquent loans/Total loans of 0.00% remain(s) below the threshold of <1.5%. Net charge-offs/Average loans of 0.00% also remain(s) within acceptable ranges.

Capital & Solvency:
Net worth ratio remains a key strength, with net worth/total assets at 10% and solvency evaluation at 110%, both remaining above their respective policy benchmarks. Net worth growth of 7% meets the target threshold of >=5% YoY.

Profitability & Growth:
Profitability metrics remain mixed, driven by efficiency pressures in operating expenses while loan yield remains favorable. Operating expenses/average assets sit at 4%, exceeding the <=3.50% limit and indicating an area to monitor for cost control. Net margin/Average assets of 4% meets its >=1.00% target, though return on average assets lags significantly below the expected benchmark.

Interest Rate & Funding:
Liquidity metrics remain within preferred ranges overall, with cash investments/total assets at 8%, total loans/total shares at 79%, and regular shares/total borrowings at 25%. However, cost of funds/Average assets remains low at 1% while yield on average loans sits comfortably above the minimum target.

Closing:
The credit union demonstrates resilience in asset quality and capital adequacy despite elevated operating expense ratios. Continued focus is needed to bring efficiency metrics back within policy limits before year-end.
